title: Timeform rating

infobox.value: is a sports data and content provider located in [Halifax, West Yorkshire](/wiki/Halifax,_West_Yorkshire "Halifax, West Yorkshire"), England. Founded in 1948, it provides systematic information on form to [punters](/wiki/Gambling "Gambling") and others involved in the [horse racing](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") industry. The company was purchased by the [sports betting](/wiki/Sports_betting "Sports betting") exchange [Betfair](/wiki/Betfair "Betfair") in December 2006. Since 2 February 2016, it has been owned by [Flutter Entertainment](/wiki/Flutter_Entertainment "Flutter Entertainment").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Timeform rating](/w/index.php?title=Timeform_rating&redirect=no "Timeform rating"))

Sports data and content provider in England

Timeform

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Company type | [Subsidiary](/wiki/Subsidiary "Subsidiary") |
| Industry | Sports data and content provider |
| Founded | 1948 |
| Headquarters | [Halifax, West Yorkshire](/wiki/Halifax,_West_Yorkshire "Halifax, West Yorkshire") |
| Owner | [Flutter Entertainment](/wiki/Flutter_Entertainment "Flutter Entertainment") |
| Website | [Timeform](http://www.timeform.com) |

**Timeform** is a sports data and content provider located in [Halifax, West Yorkshire](/wiki/Halifax,_West_Yorkshire "Halifax, West Yorkshire"), England. Founded in 1948, it provides systematic information on form to [punters](/wiki/Gambling "Gambling") and others involved in the [horse racing](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") industry. The company was purchased by the [sports betting](/wiki/Sports_betting "Sports betting") exchange [Betfair](/wiki/Betfair "Betfair") in December 2006. Since 2 February 2016, it has been owned by [Flutter Entertainment](/wiki/Flutter_Entertainment "Flutter Entertainment").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Timeform&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Portway Press Ltd was formed in 1948 by [Phil Bull](/wiki/Phil_Bull "Phil Bull"), who wanted to establish a [mathematical](/wiki/Mathematical "Mathematical") link to a horse's performance, based on the time the horse recorded. At a time when such data was virtually unheard of, Bull started publishing a racing annual, which evolved into the "Racehorses Of.." series. The company was purchased for a reputed £15 million by the sports betting exchange [Betfair](/wiki/Betfair "Betfair") in December 2006.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Data system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Timeform&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Data system")]

According to Timeform, one of its ratings represents "the merit of the horse expressed in pounds and is arrived at by careful examination of its running against other horses using a scale of weight for distance beaten which ranges from around 3 lb a length at five furlongs and 2 lb a length at a mile and a quarter to 1 lb a length at two miles".[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Timeform ratings for three-year-olds and up on the Flat:[[3]](#cite_note-3)

* 140+: an all-time great horse
* 135–139: an outstanding horse
* 130–134: above average Group 1 winner (a "top-class racehorse")[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* 125–129: average Group 1 winner (a "high-class performer")[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* 116–124: average Group 2 winner
* 110–115: average Group 3 winner
* 100–105: average Listed Race winner

Timeform states that the very poorest horses may be rated as low as 30, with the very best horses rated 130 and above.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Two-year-old ratings are slightly lower than those for older horses. It also notes that only a very select number of horses have achieved a rating of 175 and above for hurdling (16) or 182 and above for chasing (20).[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Flat vs hurdle vs steeplechase

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Timeform&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Flat vs hurdle vs steeplechase")]

Timeform maintains different scales for horses racing on the flat, over hurdles and over fences. The scores cannot be compared for the obvious differences between the race types. For instance, [Arkle](/wiki/Arkle "Arkle") at 212, [Flyingbolt](/wiki/Flyingbolt "Flyingbolt") at 210, [Sprinter Sacre](/wiki/Sprinter_Sacre "Sprinter Sacre") at 192 are then followed by [Mill House](/wiki/Mill_House_(horse) "Mill House (horse)") and [Kauto Star](/wiki/Kauto_Star "Kauto Star"), both at 191, are the highest rated steeplechasers. The highest rated horses over hurdles are [Night Nurse](/wiki/Night_Nurse_(horse) "Night Nurse (horse)") at 182, [Istabraq](/wiki/Istabraq "Istabraq") and [Monksfield](/wiki/Monksfield "Monksfield"), both at 180, and [Persian War](/wiki/Persian_War_(horse) "Persian War (horse)") at 179. The table below lists scores for flat horses only starting with the highest, rated at 147 which is the British horse [Frankel](/wiki/Frankel_(horse) "Frankel (horse)").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Timeform&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Publications")]

Annually in March, the company puts out its book *Racehorses of ....*, which currently contains more than 1,200 pages and provides information and ratings on the top flat horses in [Australasia](/wiki/Australasia "Australasia"), [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai"), France, Germany, Hong Kong, Italy, Japan, North America and [Scandinavia](/wiki/Scandinavia "Scandinavia"). Also, annually in October the company publishes *Chasers and Hurdlers*, a thorough review of every horse that ran under [National Hunt](/wiki/National_Hunt "National Hunt") rules in Britain, as well as the best National Hunt horses to have run in Ireland and France.

Timeform publishes a number of other books. Its *Horses to Follow: Flat Season* is composed of short essays on fifty horses deemed worthy of following in the upcoming year's classics. The horses are selected by Timeform's own team of experts along with five leading broadcasters. The book also provides a guide to the most significant two-year-old performances from the previous year.

Timeform also compiles a system based on speed and class which appears in the [Daily Racing Form](/wiki/Daily_Racing_Form "Daily Racing Form") for all [horse racing](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") events run in the United Kingdom, Ireland, France and the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates").

Until very recently (approximately 2000), horses who raced exclusively in America were never given Timeform ratings, so they can not be compared directly to their European counterparts.
